summary: Excellent audience recording with full bass and clean highs captures an energetic show highlighted by the China Cat Sunflower→Crazy Fingers→Rider combo and a stellar Playing in the Band.
review: Russ’s audience capture delivers a crisp, front‑of‑house feel with balanced mids and tight low end, making the dusty July ’88 performance easy to enjoy.  The concert is praised for a strong first set, an exciting second‑set sequence (China Cat Sunflower → Crazy Fingers → Rider) and a superb, Brent‑era rendition of Playing in the Band, though one reviewer felt the overall show was only fair compared to peak ’70s shows.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is consistently described as fantastic/excellent; performance is mostly praised with several standout moments, offset by a single moderate review, yielding an overall strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Up‑front FOB sound, full bass, clean highs, very balanced mix; one note about inverted order of the two Space tracks but no audible defects.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > Crazy Fingers > Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong first set followed by a lively second‑set combo; the unique China Cat Sunflower→Crazy Fingers→Rider sequence is highlighted, and Playing in the Band delivers a rare Brent‑era front‑to‑back performance.  Overall the show has energetic, dusty vibes with some ups and downs noted.
